text.title: Agent On-Call Runbook Template Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the agent-on-call-runbook-template methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale. The terminal `skip-this-methodology` rule is the safety valve invoked by the decision tree when preconditions fail.

rule.statement: Every step MUST default to read-only tools; mutations require explicit tier-2 approval.
rule.rationale: Auto-mutating agents amplify incident blast radius.

rule.statement: Each step MUST carry a stable `step_id`; reorder triggers a new version.
Each step MUST name the tool tier (tier-1 read / tier-2 mutate / tier-3 destructive).
Final step MUST escalate to named human if outcome != resolved.
rule.rationale: Lost escalations turn MTTR into hours.

rule.statement: Each step's output MUST attach evidence (log slice, query result, screenshot URL).
Author MUST stop and route elsewhere when the decision-tree resolves to a skip leaf; partial application of this methodology against an unfit input is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Forcing the methodology onto a mismatched input produces low-quality artefacts and erodes trust in the corpus.
